summary: Russian forces attacked two Ukrainian Sea Search and Rescue vessels conducting humanitarian missions, causing casualties. Multiple reactive and guided UAVs were reported over Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and the Black Sea region. Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches were announced from Taganrog, Bryansk and Rostov areas.
key_points: Russian attacks on two Sea Search and Rescue vessels in the Ukrainian maritime corridor resulted in casualties and evacuation efforts (Source: @UkraineNow/81741).
Reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv near Rіvnopillya, over Kharkiv near Staryi Saltiv, and over Sumy near Krоlevets (Source: @eRadarrua/95722; @kpszsu/64245; @eRadarrua/95660).
Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches were issued from Taganrog (Rostov region) and Bryansk region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508; @mon1tor_ua/67510).
Ukrainian forces reported strikes on energy infrastructure, fuel stations and postal transport in Sumy region, causing power outages (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Ukrainian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east axis, with warnings of possible air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64259).
A guided UAV from the Black Sea was tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk (Source: @kpszsu/64246; @kpszsu/64252).
Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov reiterated Russia’s demand for “full restoration of rights” for Russian‑speaking citizens in Ukraine (Source: @UkraineNow/81736).

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces attacked two Sea Search and Rescue vessels, causing casualties and prompting evacuation. Multiple reactive UAVs were reported over northern and eastern Ukrainian oblasts, while threats of Iskander‑M missile launches emerged from Taganrog and Bryansk. Ukrainian energy infrastructure in Sumy suffered attacks, and tactical aviation activity increased on the north‑east front.

points: Two Sea Search and Rescue vessels conducting humanitarian missions in the Ukrainian maritime corridor were struck, resulting in casualties and ongoing evacuation by Ukrainian Naval forces (Source: @UkraineNow/81741).

Ukrainian energy infrastructure in Sumy region, including fuel stations and postal transport, was attacked, causing localized power outages that utilities are working to restore (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Guided UAVs were observed over the Black Sea heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, indicating continued Russian aerial pressure on southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/64246; @kpszsu/64252).
Ukrainian forces reported a guided UAV over Zaporizhzhia from the south, suggesting possible targeting of industrial assets (Source: @kpszsu/64238).
A Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" operated over the Black Sea near Vylkove, monitoring positions in Sumy and Zaporizhzhia oblasts (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
Russian authorities warned of imminent Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog in Rostov region (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508).
A similar threat of Iskander‑M launches was issued from Bryansk region, heightening concerns for Ukrainian western and central fronts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67510).

Russian Foreign Minister Lavrov stated Russia will pursue “full restoration of rights” for Russian‑speaking citizens in Ukraine, linking it to long‑term conflict resolution (Source: @UkraineNow/81736).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east axis, with warnings of possible air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64259).
Russian forces continued systematic attacks on the transport link between Kharkiv and Sumy, as noted by Ukrainian sources (Source: @UkraineNow/81743).
Reactive UAVs were sighted over Chernihiv near Rіvnopillya, indicating Russian aerial activity in the north‑east (Source: @eRadarrua/95722).
Ukrainian intelligence reported infantry battles in Shostka district (Sumy) with advances of up to 700 m in multiple sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian forces engaged GRU “North” units in Bachevsk and surrounding areas of Sumy, with counter‑attacks repelled near Ulanovo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian drone attacks targeted logistics centers near Kachiy Lopan’ on the Kharkiv front, with fierce firefights reported in Shevchenko and Volchansk sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" monitored positions north of Stepanivka and other points in Sumy oblast (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
A hostile reconnaissance UAV was detected over northern Chernihiv oblast, highlighting Russian surveillance north of Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu/64227).
Reactive UAVs were observed over Kharkiv oblast near Staryi Saltiv, moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/64245).
Ukrainian forces reported a guided UAV approaching Zaporizhzhia from the south, suggesting potential strikes on industrial targets (Source: @kpszsu/64238).
Tactical aviation activity was warned on the north‑east direction, raising the risk of air strikes on frontline settlements (Source: @kpszsu/64259).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a UAV heading toward Poltava from the east, indicating possible reconnaissance over central Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/64251).
Guided UAVs from the Black Sea were tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, indicating pressure on southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/64246; @kpszsu/64252).
Ukrainian energy facilities in Sumy, though not directly on the southern front, faced attacks that could affect supply lines to the south (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Russian attacks on maritime rescue vessels in the Black Sea corridor underscore threats to humanitarian shipping (Source: @UkraineNow/81741).
Ukrainian forces reported a guided UAV over Zaporizhzhia from the south, potentially targeting industrial infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu/64238).
No specific ground engagements were detailed for the southern front in the provided messages.
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the north‑east axis, with warnings of possible air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64259).

Multiple reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Sumy and Poltava, indicating extensive Russian drone deployment across the theater (Source: @eRadarrua/95722; @kpszsu/64245; @kpszsu/64251).
Ukrainian forces reported guided UAVs operating over the Black Sea toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, suggesting Russian aerial pressure on southern maritime zones (Source: @kpszsu/64246; @kpszsu/64252).
Ukrainian reconnaissance UAV "Zala" conducted surveillance over the Black Sea near Vylkove, indicating Ukrainian aerial activity in contested waters (Source: @eRadarrua/95661).
Threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog and Bryansk imply potential integration of air‑defence and missile interception assets (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508; @mon1tor_ua/67510).

key_developments: Russian forces struck two Sea Search and Rescue vessels conducting humanitarian missions in the Ukrainian maritime corridor, causing casualties and prompting evacuation by Ukrainian naval units (Source: @UkraineNow/81741).

Reactive UAVs were observed over Chernihiv near Rіvnopillya, over Kharkiv near Staryi Saltiv, and over Sumy near Krоlevets, indicating expanded Russian drone activity in northern and eastern Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/95722; @kpszsu/64245; @eRadarrua/95660).

Russian authorities issued threats of Iskander‑M ballistic missile launches from Taganrog in Rostov region and from Bryansk region, raising the risk of long‑range strikes (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67508; @mon1tor_ua/67510).
Ukrainian energy infrastructure in Sumy region, including fuel stations and postal transport, was attacked, causing localized power outages (Source: @UkraineNow/81732).
Ukrainian intelligence reported infantry battles in Sumy’s Shostka district with Ukrainian forces advancing up to 700 m in multiple sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30722).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on the north‑east direction, with warnings of possible air strikes on frontline areas (Source: @kpszsu/64259).
Guided UAVs from the Black Sea were tracked heading toward Odesa and Chornomorsk, indicating continued Russian aerial pressure on southern ports (Source: @kpszsu/64246; @kpszsu/64252).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian drone proliferation across northern, eastern and southern fronts threatens both civilian infrastructure and military logistics, while the announced Iskander‑M missile threats from Taganrog and Bryansk could enable long‑range strikes against strategic targets. Ukrainian energy sites in Sumy remain vulnerable, and maritime rescue operations face direct attacks, jeopardizing humanitarian corridors. Continued Russian tactical aviation activity on the north‑east axis heightens the risk of air assaults on frontline positions.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are conducting evacuations of damaged maritime vessels and reinforcing air‑defence systems against the reported UAV influx. Energy utilities in Sumy are mobilising repair crews to restore power, and ground forces are consolidating gains in Sumy’s infantry battles. International partners have noted the escalation, but no new aid commitments were detailed in the provided messages.
sections.intelligence_notes: The concentration of reactive UAVs near key transport hubs suggests a Russian focus on disrupting logistics and reconnaissance. The dual threats of UAVs and Iskander‑M missiles require integrated air‑defence and early‑warning capabilities. Monitoring of Black Sea drone trajectories toward Odesa and Chornomorsk is critical to protect maritime supply lines and civilian vessels.
